what_it_does: It is a build implementation guide for the recollection episodic-memory plugin, specifying governing principles, per-component schema/spec, file changes, model-slot bindings, do/don't rules, build phasing, and documented silent-failure corrections.
observations: Defines registry-is-truth / no-hardcoding as a governing principle across unit types, lenses, judges, capture sources, and edge types.
Specifies SQLite+sqlite-vec schema additions: atoms, units, a unit_type registry, links, fingerprints, verdicts, and candidates.
Calls for replacing the cloud-based summarizer with a local resident-4B distill pipeline via fabric.
Interim embedding stack targets served endpoints at 127.0.0.1:8007 and :8008, using pplx-ctx-4b-docs with dim 2560 and cosine comparison.
Separates extraction (workers) from judgment (central judge) and adds a principle-ratification gate for Tim's principles.
Introduces a questionless-sweep lane for proactive inventory, merge-as-join matching, and orphan detection.

Includes a wave-3 build-readiness corrections section listing silent-failure landmines such as sidechain filters, NULL tool_result, committed dist, env allowlist, SessionStart recursion, vec0 dim rebuild, and the 13k head-start caveat.
Adopts an embedding-axis registry and enforced json_schema structured outputs in its fabric-aligned notes.
standouts: The file doubles as both a design spec and a risk register for silent build failures.
It insists on registry-driven, no-hardcoding design for every extensible concept.
It applies a bootstrap rule: the build uses its own recollection capability to recover decisions for later phases.
It elevates no-fiction-about-Tim to a first-class constraint with an explicit ratification gate.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with Principle/Schema/Files/Model-slot bindings/Do's & Don'ts/Preserves structure.
Uses a tense convention: IS means exists today; WILL BE means to build.
Uses a KEEP/MODIFY/REPLACE/NEW/HARVEST taxonomy for file changes.
Repeatedly references external files (RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md, COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md, OPEN-DECISIONS.md) as evidence and truth tables.
Registry-is-truth pattern is applied consistently to unit types, lenses, judges, capture sources, distill layers, and edge types.
concerns: Dense references to unprovided files make standalone verification impossible.
Operational interim endpoint details (ports 8007/8008) may drift and are not abstracted behind a single config surface in the visible text.
A large number of explicit DON'T rules indicates a high compliance surface and prior failure modes.
Phase codes (D-1, D-2, D-5, P3-P5, P8) and some terms are used without definition in this file.
notes: A detailed, risk-aware implementation guide for a registry-driven episodic memory system, with explicit anti-patterns and silent-failure corrections.
questions: What are the exact contents of COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md, RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md, and OPEN-DECISIONS.md that this file treats as truth tables?
What do the phase codes D-1, D-2, D-5, P3-P5, and P8 specifically denote?
What is the current codebase state relative to the IS/WILL BE tense markers?
How is migration handled from the existing ~/.config/superpowers data dir to ~/.recollection?
What is the precise workflow and UI for the principle ratification gate?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a per-component implementation guide and then provides principles, schema/spec, files, model slots, do/don't rules, preserves, phasing, and risk corrections for each component.
intention_signals: It is trying to turn a v1.4.2 clone into a standalone, portable episodic-memory plugin by specifying exactly what to build, what to preserve, and what prior mistakes to avoid, while making the system self-extending through registries and self-verifying through use.
novelty: The combination of a registry-is-truth data model, a multi-axis embedding registry with no privileged model, a questionless-sweep proactive recall lane, and an embedded silent-failure audit section within one implementation guide.
contribution: A structured, risk-aware build plan that ties schema changes, model-slot assignments, file modifications, and anti-patterns to completion criteria, preserving existing capture/render behavior while adding units, links, fingerprints, verdicts, and registry-driven lenses/judges.
summary_for_embedding: This file is an implementation guide for building the recollection episodic-memory plugin, covering schema, capture, distill, embed, link, gather, judge, recall, health, build phasing, and known silent-failure corrections.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to direct a concrete, registry-driven build of a portable episodic-memory system while preserving existing functionality and avoiding documented failure modes.
